frameworks.name: model-based inference
frameworks.one_line: one-trial context switching from a single unexpected outcome

frameworks.name: neural population geometry
frameworks.one_line: curvilinear decision manifolds displaced along the decision-variable axis

frameworks.name: recurrent neural network
frameworks.one_line: RNNs trained on the task reproduce behavior and geometric shifts

frameworks.name: evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: decision variable accumulates sensory evidence prior to choice

core_question: How is reward context learned, represented, and updated in prefrontal populations during perceptual decision making?
key_assumptions: latent reward context must be inferred from outcomes
a fixed linear readout can decode choice across contexts
decision variable and choice can be mapped onto low-dimensional manifolds
manipulations: reward contingency favoring leftward vs rightward choices, switched unpredictably
learning stage (early vs trained) within longitudinal recordings
comparison of monkey neural data with task-matched RNN models
key_findings: trained monkeys infer context switches from one unexpected outcome and adjust bias and RT
early in learning, adaptation unfolds gradually across many trials
reward context shifts neural manifold geometry primarily along the decision-variable axis
with learning, transitions between context manifolds become faster
interpretations: context-dependent biases arise from geometric displacement of decision manifolds rather than remapping
the speed of representational transitions tracks the emergence of one-trial behavioral generalization
